title: Withdraw 25,400,000 ada for Intersect: Governance coordination and technical ...

rationale.summary: Vote: NEEDS_MORE_INFO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), critical information needed for a responsible directional vote is still missing. Decisive reason: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "Intersect says the proposal funds its core operating model for June 2026 to June 2027, including governance coordination, reserved capacity for critical unowned processes, and technical stewardship for Cardano." and clears the evidence gates. What would enable a directional vote: Provide: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence; Provide sustainability path. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score 0.18; confidence 76.6%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is material but below threshold; treated as a modest context signal.
top_fixes: Provide: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence
missing_evidence: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Withdraw treasury funds for Intersect governance coordination, technical stewardship, incident response, release coordination, repository management, security initiatives, ecosystem coordination, and management of critical processes for June 2026 to June 2027.
Recipient: Intersect via the 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v.

Deliverables: WP 1 - Intersect operations and ecosystem coordination: 6,000,000 ADA, WP 2 - Technical Stewardship, Incident Response & Coordination, including core Cardano repositories: 18,800,000 ADA, WP 3 - Management of critical processes: 600,000 ADA, Milestone-based drawdowns, evidence-backed reporting, transparent disclosures, and external assurance process

Claim (other, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Intersect says the proposal funds its core operating model for June 2026 to June 2027, including governance coordination, reserved capacity for critical unowned processes, and technical stewardship for Cardano.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The requested budget totals 25,400,000 ADA, split into 6,000,000 ADA for operations and ecosystem coordination, 18,800,000 ADA for technical stewardship and incident response, and 600,000 ADA for management of critical processes.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Intersect states its overall ask has decreased from $7.875M last year to $6.35M while preserving the functions it considers most critical to Cardano continuity and maturity.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Intersect claims it has already coordinated two network upgrades, is preparing for a third, and led ecosystem coordination during the November 2025 chain partition incident.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): The proposal says it primarily supports infrastructure and research excellence, governance, and ecosystem sustainability and resilience, with secondary support for adoption, utility, community, and ecosystem growth.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal states it achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase and references checkable voting documentation and audited results.
missing: Independent evidence for: Intersect states its overall ask has decreased from $7.875M last year to $6.35M while preserving the functions it considers most critical to Cardano continuity and maturity.
Independent evidence for: Intersect claims it has already coordinated two network upgrades, is preparing for a third, and led ecosystem coordination during the November 2025 chain partition incident.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Intersect says the proposal funds its core operating model for June 2026 to June 2027, including governance coordination, reserved capacity for critical unowned processes, and technical stewardship for Cardano." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Intersect states its overall ask has decreased from $7.875M last year to $6.35M while preserving the functions it considers most critical to Cardano continuity and maturity." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

actionable_asks: State the cost against the benefit: what does this buy, per ada spent.
Describe the funding path beyond this grant, or say plainly that there isn't one.
Disclose the rate basis — headcount, FTE fraction, or day rate — so the price can be checked against the market.
Tie payment to defined deliverables, not to elapsed time or headcount.
State your AI-cost assumption: this work is cheaper to produce than it was, and the price should show it.
missing: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
